73996.subject: 📉 Bitcoin crashes as altcoins show strength
73996.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $67,254 (-3.1%) | **ETH** $1,883 (-4.7%) | **SOL** $76 (-4.3%)

* Bitcoin drops [below $66K](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2062015515631964601?s=20) as many [alts outperform](https://x.com/Tyler_Did_It/status/2062127683287261348?s=20) — BTC ETFs record [12th straight day](https://x.com/solidintel_x/status/2062086204506452408?s=20) of outflows

* S&P 500 hits [$69T market cap](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2061845315401998592?s=20) for first time

* Trump calls Netanyahu [“f*cking crazy”](https://x.com/DeItaone/status/2062116929716760906?s=20) during strategy call — Signs executive order on [AI innovation and security ](https://x.com/Cointelegraph/status/2061835979963674635?s=20)

* Strive [buys $185M](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2061793235564110020?s=20) worth of Bitcoin

* Hermes launches [desktop app](https://x.com/NousResearch/status/2061843507417944552?s=20) for their agent framework

* Zcash network briefly [stops producing blocks](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2062137435849461853?s=20) as it releases emergency update to fix bug

* US tech stocks have strongest [2 month rally](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2061839362090762442?s=20) since dot com bubble

* Brian Armstrong’s longevity startup raises [$345M Series C](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2062038210784776554?s=20) round

* Wall Street Journal publishes [article](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2062018665252639227?s=20) about Hyperliquid’s 24/7 trading platform

* [UK football clubs](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2062053988414235069?s=20) face legal and money laundering risks from crypto sponsors

* Microsoft announces [“Scout”](https://decrypt.co/369781/microsoft-scout-openclaw-enterprise-ai-agent) autopilot agent

* [Clarity Act added](https://x.com/WatcherGuru/status/2061864525192659312?s=20) to Senate legislative calendar

* Polymarket completes [6 figure on-chain institutional hedge](https://x.com/Cointelegraph/status/2061857877925786081?s=20) tied to Nvidia GPU compute prices

* Backpack announces upcoming launch of [“Backpack Securities”](https://x.com/DegenerateNews/status/2061814494456603129?s=20)

* Anthropic releases [Project Glasswing](https://x.com/MTSlive/status/2061807595640553

73995.subject: What is Bybit AI subaccount? A beginner's guide
73995.body:
Bybit Learn

Автор: Bybit


Автор: Bybit

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/what-bybit-ai-subaccount-beginners-guide-bybitexchange-i6fpe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B3Zq9JTFOTcex23dWKxhW%2Fg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3WGEkzwsEYPsg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpy10a9x~79-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpy10a9x-79
  
AI-powered trading agents have moved from experimental tooling to a practical reality for retail traders, with major exchanges now building dedicated infrastructure to accommodate them. Bybit has responded with a purpose-built account type, the AI Subaccount, designed specifically for AI agents to execute trades through API access. Authentication works via public/private key pairs, withdrawals are disabled by default and asset exposure is capped at $5,000 unless you change this amount.  In this article, we describe the AI Subaccount and show you how it differs from a regular Bybit Subaccount, how to authenticate it and how to set it up on both the web and the Bybit App. Key Takeaways: The Bybit AI Subaccount is a purpose-built, API-only account type that isolates AI agent activity from your primary funds and other Subaccounts. Authentication relies on a public/private key exchange, rather than a password. API keys expire every 30 days by default. Asset exposure is capped at $5,000 by default, with granular permission controls managed from the parent account. What is the Bybit AI Subaccount? The Bybit AI Subaccount is a segregated account type designed exclusively for AI assistants to interact with the exchange on your behalf via API. Whereas a Standard Subaccount is a trading environment a human can log into directly to execute trades, the AI Subaccount’s activity is driven by your linked AI assistant. There’s no password-based login, and no in-app account switching into it. AI agents authenticate through a public/private key pair, rather than via login credentials, which changes the security model significantly. All trades, transfers and activity generated by the connected agent stay within the Subaccount’s isolated environment, with no capability to move assets into your Main Account (or other Subaccounts) without explicit permission. Bybit treats the AI agent as a separate, bounded identity, rather than an extension of the account holder. How does it differ from a regular Subaccount? The core difference between a Standard Subaccount and an AI Subaccount depends upon whom the account is built for and how access is granted. Why use an AI Subaccount? Separation of funds from your Main Account. The AI Subaccount operates in a ringfenced environment, meaning that your primary holdings and other Subaccounts are never exposed to agent activity. Built-in cap li

Fear and Greed is at
27
(Fear).
BTC fell to $66K, extending its slide below the $68K level it had
briefly steadied at, now sitting at its lowest point since early
April. Eleven straight days of ETF outflows have pulled $3.45
billion from Bitcoin products, and fresh capital has effectively
stopped arriving on-chain according to Glassnode.

The pressure is not random. Institutional money is chasing
record equity highs instead. Strategy's surprise Bitcoin sale
shifted the narrative. And beneath the selloff, a structural
debate is building about whether Ethereum has a fundamental
advantage over Bitcoin that the market is only beginning to price
in.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Standard Chartered Says ETH Is Starting to Outperform BTC

Standard Chartered called June 1 the start of Ethereum
outperforming Bitcoin, triggered by Strategy's Bitcoin sale. ETH
rose roughly 5% against BTC even as crypto broadly fell.

ETH generates staking yield near 3% annually. Bitcoin treasury
firms earn nothing and may need to sell to meet their
obligations.

What ETH to BTC ratio does Standard Chartered expect by
year-end?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/6jCy566cd9v2s99WeANUvw~~/AAQRxRA~/UNdfxqrlXeI5r03mfnl8-9VAcWWPKR5UcURR6eN4rNK5Gz2zZXAuFLR5uGf-4zKb37RND-qwMX14mTnXWifMrXKUHSpvT9_0WTXx0jHpvpvZJNJFRtrZJYGYQeJJ_4pabRGSr8Td7crg8amOrfcxGRK6WDd7EHaVWzFBa5myGPyNDtDqaaxUN8qJlDYoBb
